1278: static void gf_m2ts_section_complete(GF_M2TS_Demuxer *ts, GF_M2TS_SectionFilter *sec, GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *ses)
1279: {
1280: 	if (ts->seek_mode && (sec->section[0] != 0x00) && (sec->section[0] != 0x02)) {
1281: 		if (sec->section) gf_free(sec->section);
1282: 		sec->section = NULL;
1283: 		sec->length = sec->received = 0;
1284: 		return;
1285: 	}
1286: 
1287: 	if (!sec->process_section) {
1288: 		if ((ts->on_event && (sec->section[0]==0x74)) ) {
1289: 			GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
1290: 			pck.data_len = sec->length;
1291: 			pck.data = sec->section;
1292: 			pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
1293: 		} else if ((ts->on_event && (sec->section[0]==0x3A || sec->section[0]==0x3B ||
1294: 		                             sec->section[0]==0x3C || sec->section[0]==0x3D || sec->section[0]==0x3E)) ) {
1295: 
1296: 			GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
1297: 			pck.data_len = sec->length;
1298: 			pck.data = sec->section;
1299: 			pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
1300: 		}
1301: 		else if ((ts->on_event && (sec->section[0]==0xFC)) ) {
1302: 			GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
1303: 			pck.data_len = sec->length;
1304: 			pck.data = sec->section;
1305: 			pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
1306: 			ts->on_event(ts, 30, &pck);
1307: 		}
1308: 		else if (ts->on_event) {
1309: 			GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
1310: 			pck.data_len = sec->length;
1311: 			pck.data = sec->section;
1312: 			pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
1313: 			ts->on_event(ts, 24, &pck);
1314: 		}
1315: 	} else {
1316: 		Bool has_syntax_indicator;
1317: 		u8 table_id;
1318: 		u16 extended_table_id;
1319: 		u32 status, section_start, i;
1320: 		GF_M2TS_Table *t, *prev_t;
1321: 		unsigned char *data;
1322: 		Bool section_valid = 0;
1323: 
1324: 		status = 0;
1325: 		data = (u8 *)sec->section;
1326: 
1327: 		if (sec->length < 2) {
1328: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] M2TS Table parsing error, length %d is too small\n", sec->length) );
1329: 			return;
1330: 		}
1331: 
1332: 		table_id = data[0];
1333: 
1334: 		if (ts->on_event) {
1335: 			switch (table_id) {
1336: 			case 0x00:
1337: 			case 0x42:
1338: 			case 0x02:
1339: 			case 0x40:
1340: 			case 0x70:
1341: 			case 0x73:
1342: 			{
1343: 				GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
1344: 				pck.data_len = sec->length;
1345: 				pck.data = sec->section;
1346: 				pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
1347: 				ts->on_event(ts, 24, &pck);
1348: 			}
1349: 			}
1350: 		}
1351: 
1352: 		has_syntax_indicator = (data[1] & 0x80) ? 1 : 0;
1353: 		if (has_syntax_indicator) {
1354: 			if (sec->length < 5) {
1355: 				GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] M2TS Table parsing error, length %d is too small\n", sec->length) );
1356: 				return;
1357: 			}
1358: 			extended_table_id = (data[3]<<8) | data[4];
1359: 		} else {
1360: 			extended_table_id = 0;
1361: 		}
1362: 
1363: 		prev_t = NULL;
1364: 		t = sec->table;
1365: 		while (t) {
1366: 			if ((t->table_id==table_id) && (t->ex_table_id == extended_table_id)) break;
1367: 			prev_t = t;
1368: 			t = t->next;
1369: 		}
1370: 
1371: 		if (!t) {
1372: 			GF_SAFEALLOC(t, GF_M2TS_Table);
1373: 			if (!t) {
1374: 				GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] Fail to alloc table %d %d\n", table_id, extended_table_id));
1375: 				return;
1376: 			}
1377: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] Creating table %d %d\n", table_id, extended_table_id));
1378: 			t->table_id = table_id;
1379: 			t->ex_table_id = extended_table_id;
1380: 			t->last_version_number = 0xFF;
1381: 			t->sections = gf_list_new();
1382: 			if (prev_t) prev_t->next = t;
1383: 			else sec->table = t;
1384: 		}
1385: 
1386: 		if (has_syntax_indicator) {
1387: 			if (sec->length < 4) {
1388: 				GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] corrupted section length %d less than CRC \n", sec->length));
1389: 			} else {
1390: 				sec->length -= 4;
1391: 				if (1) {
1392: 					s32 cur_sec_num;
1393: 					t->version_number = (data[5] >> 1) & 0x1f;
1394: 					if (t->last_section_number && t->section_number && (t->version_number != t->last_version_number)) {
1395: 						GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] table transmission interrupted: previous table (v=%d) %d/%d sections - new table (v=%d) %d/%d sections\n", t->last_version_number, t->section_number, t->last_section_number, t->version_number, data[6] + 1, data[7] + 1) );
1396: 						t->section_number = 0;
1397: 					}
1398: 
1399: 					t->current_next_indicator = (data[5] & 0x1) ? 1 : 0;
1400: 					cur_sec_num = data[6] + 1;
1401: 					t->last_section_number = data[7] + 1;
1402: 					section_start = 8;
1403: 					if (!sec->process_individual && t->section_number + 1 != cur_sec_num) {
1404: 						section_valid = 0;
1405: 						if (t->is_init) {
1406: 							GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] corrupted table (lost section %d)\n", cur_sec_num ? cur_sec_num-1 : 31) );
1407: 						}
1408: 					} else {
1409: 						section_valid = 1;
1410: 						t->section_number = cur_sec_num;
1411: 					}
1412: 				} else {
1413: 					GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] corrupted section (CRC32 failed)\n"));
1414: 				}
1415: 			}
1416: 		} else {
1417: 			section_valid = 1;
1418: 			section_start = 3;
1419: 		}
1420: 		if (section_valid) {
1421: 			GF_M2TS_Section *section;
1422: 
1423: 			GF_SAFEALLOC(section, GF_M2TS_Section);
1424: 			if (!section) {
1425: 				GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] Fail to create section\n"));
1426: 				return;
1427: 			}
1428: 			section->data_size = sec->length - section_start;
1429: 			section->data = (unsigned char*)gf_malloc(sizeof(unsigned char)*section->data_size);
1430: 			memcpy(section->data, sec->section + section_start, sizeof(unsigned char)*section->data_size);
1431: 			gf_list_add(t->sections, section);
1432: 
1433: 			if (t->section_number == 1) {
1434: 				status |= 1;
1435: 				if (t->last_version_number == t->version_number) {
1436: 					t->is_repeat = 1;
1437: 				} else {
1438: 					t->is_repeat = 0;
1439: 				}
1440: 				t->last_version_number = t->version_number;
1441: 			}
1442: 
1443: 			if (t->is_init) {
1444: 				if (t->is_repeat) {
1445: 					status |=  1<<3;
1446: 				} else {
1447: 					status |=  1<<2;
1448: 				}
1449: 			} else {
1450: 				status |=  1<<1;
1451: 			}
1452: 
1453: 			if (t->last_section_number == t->section_number) {
1454: 				u32 table_size;
1455: 
1456: 				status |= 1<<0;
1457: 
1458: 				table_size = 0;
1459: 				for (i=0; i<gf_list_count(t->sections); i++) {
1460: 					GF_M2TS_Section *a_sec = gf_list_get(t->sections, i);
1461: 					table_size += a_sec->data_size;
1462: 				}
1463: 				if (t->is_repeat) {
1464: 					if (t->table_size != table_size) {
1465: 						status |= 1<<2;
1466: 						GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] Repeated section found with different sizes (old table %d bytes, new table %d bytes)\n", t->table_size, table_size) );
1467: 
1468: 						t->table_size = table_size;
1469: 					}
1470: 				} else {
1471: 					t->table_size = table_size;
1472: 				}
1473: 
1474: 				t->is_init = 1;
1475: 				t->section_number = 0;
1476: 
1477: 				t->is_repeat = 0;
1478: 
1479: 			}
1480: 
1481: 			if (sec->process_individual) {
1482: 				if (sec->process_section)
1483: 					sec->process_section(ts, ses, t->sections, t->table_id, t->ex_table_id, t->version_number, (u8) (t->last_section_number - 1), status);
1484: 
1485: 				t->section_number = 0;
1486: 			} else {
1487: 				if (status& (1<<0)) {
1488: 					if (sec->process_section)
1489: 						sec->process_section(ts, ses, t->sections, t->table_id, t->ex_table_id, t->version_number, (u8) (t->last_section_number - 1), status);
1490: 
1491: 					t->section_number = 0;
1492: 				}
1493: 			}
1494: 
1495: 		} else {
1496: 			sec->cc = -1;
1497: 			t->section_number = 0;
1498: 		}
1499: 	}
1500: 	if (sec->section) gf_free(sec->section);
1501: 	sec->section = NULL;
1502: 	sec->length = sec->received = 0;
1503: }
1504: 
1505: static void gf_m2ts_gather_section(GF_M2TS_Demuxer *ts, GF_M2TS_SectionFilter *sec, GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *ses, void *hdr, unsigned char *data, u32 data_size)
1506: {
1507: 	u32 payload_size = data_size;
1508: 	u8 expect_cc = (sec->cc<0) ? ((u8*)hdr)[3] & 0xf : (sec->cc + 1) & 0xf;
1509: 	Bool disc = (expect_cc == ((u8*)hdr)[3] & 0xf) ? 0 : 1;
1510: 	sec->cc = expect_cc;
1511: 
1512: 	if (!data_size) return;
1513: 
1514: 	if (((u8*)hdr)[1] & 0x40) {
1515: 		u32 ptr_field;
1516: 
1517: 		ptr_field = data[0];
1518: 		if (ptr_field+1>data_size) {
1519: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] Invalid section start (@ptr_field=%d, @data_size=%d)\n", ptr_field, data_size) );
1520: 			return;
1521: 		}
1522: 
1523: 		if (!hdr)
1524: 			ts->last_pat_start_num = ts->pck_number-1;
1525: 
1526: 		if (!sec->length && sec->received) {
1527: 			if (sec->received == 1)
1528: 				sec->length = (sec->section[0] << 8) | data[1];
1529: 			else
1530: 				sec->length = (sec->section[0] << 8) | sec->section[1];
1531: 			sec->section = (char*)gf_realloc(sec->section, sizeof(char)*sec->length);
1532: 		}
1533: 
1534: 		if (sec->length && sec->received + ptr_field >= sec->length) {
1535: 			u32 len = sec->length - sec->received;
1536: 			memcpy(sec->section + sec->received, data+1, sizeof(char)*len);
1537: 			sec->received += len;
1538: 			if (ptr_field > len)
1539: 				GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] Invalid pointer field (@ptr_field=%d, @remaining=%d)\n", ptr_field, len) );
1540: 			gf_m2ts_section_complete(ts, sec, ses);
1541: 		}
1542: 		data += ptr_field+1;
1543: 		data_size -= ptr_field+1;
1544: 		payload_size -= ptr_field+1;
1545: 
1546: aggregated_section:
1547: 
1548: 		if (sec->section) gf_free(sec->section);
1549: 		sec->length = sec->received = 0;
1550: 		sec->section = (char*)gf_malloc(sizeof(char)*data_size);
1551: 		memcpy(sec->section, data, sizeof(char)*data_size);
1552: 		sec->received = data_size;
1553: 	} else if (disc) {
1554: 		if (sec->section) gf_free(sec->section);
1555: 		sec->section = NULL;
1556: 		sec->received = sec->length = 0;
1557: 		return;
1558: 	} else if (!sec->section) {
1559: 		return;
1560: 	} else {
1561: 		if (sec->length && sec->received+data_size > sec->length)
1562: 			data_size = sec->length - sec->received;
1563: 
1564: 		if (sec->length) {
1565: 			memcpy(sec->section + sec->received, data, sizeof(char)*data_size);
1566: 		} else {
1567: 			sec->section = (char*)gf_realloc(sec->section, sizeof(char)*(sec->received+data_size));
1568: 			memcpy(sec->section + sec->received, data, sizeof(char)*data_size);
1569: 		}
1570: 		sec->received += data_size;
1571: 	}
1572: 	if (!sec->length && (sec->received >= 3)) {
1573: 		sec->length = (sec->section[0] << 8) | sec->section[1];
1574: 		sec->section = (char*)gf_realloc(sec->section, sizeof(char)*sec->length);
1575: 
1576: 		if (sec->received > sec->length) {
1577: 			data_size -= sec->received - sec->length;
1578: 			sec->received = sec->length;
1579: 		}
1580: 	}
1581: 	if (!sec->length || sec->received < sec->length) return;
1582: 
1583: 	gf_m2ts_section_complete(ts, sec, ses);
1584: 
1585: 	if (payload_size > data_size) {
1586: 		data += data_size;
1587: 		if (data[0] != 0xFF) {
1588: 			data_size = payload_size - data_size;
1589: 			payload_size = data_size;
1590: 			goto aggregated_section;
1591: 		}
1592: 	}
1593: }
1594: 
1595: static GF_Err gf_m2ts_process_packet(GF_M2TS_Demuxer *ts, unsigned char *data)
1596: {
1597: 	GF_M2TS_ES *es;
1598: 	GF_M2TS_Header hdr;
1599: 	GF_M2TS_AdaptationField af, *paf;
1600: 	u32 payload_size, af_size;
1601: 	u32 pos = 0;
1602: 
1603: 	ts->pck_number++;
1604: 
1605: 	/* read TS packet header*/
1606: 	hdr.sync = data[0];
1607: 	if (hdr.sync != 0x47) {
1608: 		GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d does not start with sync marker\n", ts->pck_number));
1609: 		return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1610: 	}
1611: 	hdr.error = (data[1] & 0x80) ? 1 : 0;
1612: 	hdr.payload_start = (data[1] & 0x40) ? 1 : 0;
1613: 	hdr.priority = (data[1] & 0x20) ? 1 : 0;
1614: 	hdr.pid = ( (data[1]&0x1f) << 8) | data[2];
1615: 	hdr.scrambling_ctrl = (data[3] >> 6) & 0x3;
1616: 	hdr.adaptation_field = (data[3] >> 4) & 0x3;
1617: 	hdr.continuity_counter = data[3] & 0xf;
1618: 
1619: 	if (hdr.error) {
1620: 		GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d has error (PID could be %d)\n", ts->pck_number, hdr.pid));
1621: 		return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1622: 	}
1623: //#if DEBUG_TS_PACKET
1624: 	GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d PID %d CC %d Encrypted %d\n", ts->pck_number, hdr.pid, hdr.continuity_counter, hdr.scrambling_ctrl));
1625: //#endif
1626: 
1627: 	if (hdr.scrambling_ctrl) {
1628: 		//TODO add decyphering
1629: 		GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d is scrambled - not supported\n", ts->pck_number, hdr.pid));
1630: 		return GF_NOT_SUPPORTED;
1631: 	}
1632: 
1633: 	paf = NULL;
1634: 	payload_size = 184;
1635: 	pos = 4;
1636: 	switch (hdr.adaptation_field) {
1637: 	/*adaptation+data*/
1638: 	case 3:
1639: 		af_size = data[4];
1640: 		if (af_size>183) {
1641: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d AF field larger than 183  for AF type 3!\n", ts->pck_number));
1642: 			//error
1643: 			return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1644: 		}
1645: 		paf = ⁡
1646: 		memset(paf, 0, sizeof(GF_M2TS_AdaptationField));
1647: 		if (af_size) gf_m2ts_get_adaptation_field(ts, paf, data+5, af_size, hdr.pid);
1648: 		pos += 1+af_size;
1649: 		payload_size = 183 - af_size;
1650: 		break;
1651: 	/*adaptation only - still process in case of PCR*/
1652: 	case 2:
1653: 		af_size = data[4];
1654: 		if (af_size != 183) {
1655: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d AF size is %d when it must be 183 for AF type 2\n", ts->pck_number, af_size));
1656: 			return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1657: 		}
1658: 		paf = ⁡
1659: 		memset(paf, 0, sizeof(GF_M2TS_AdaptationField));
1660: 		gf_m2ts_get_adaptation_field(ts, paf, data+5, af_size, hdr.pid);
1661: 		payload_size = 0;
1662: 		/*no payload and no PCR, return*/
1663: 		if (!paf->PCR_flag)
1664: 			return GF_OK;
1665: 		break;
1666: 	/*reserved*/
1667: 	case 0:
1668: 		return GF_OK;
1669: 	default:
1670: 		break;
1671: 	}
1672: 	data += pos;
1673: 
1674: 	/*PAT*/
1675: 	if (hdr.pid == GF_M2TS_PID_PAT) {
1676: 		gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ts->pat, NULL, &hdr, data, payload_size);
1677: 		return GF_OK;
1678: 	}
1679: 
1680: 	es = ts->ess[hdr.pid];
1681: 	//we work in split mode
1682: 	if (ts->split_mode) {
1683: 		GF_M2TS_TSPCK tspck;
1684: 		//process PMT table
1685: 		if (es && (es->flags & GF_M2TS_ES_IS_PMT)) {
1686: 			GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *ses = (GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *)es;
1687: 			if (ses->sec) gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ses->sec, ses, &hdr, data, payload_size);
1688: 		}
1689: 		//and forward every packet other than PAT
1690: 		tspck.stream = es;
1691: 		tspck.pid = hdr.pid;
1692: 		tspck.data = data - pos;
1693: 		ts->on_event(ts, GF_M2TS_EVT_PCK, &tspck);
1694: 		return GF_OK;
1695: 	}
1696: 
1697: 	if (hdr.pid == GF_M2TS_PID_CAT) {
1698: 		gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ts->cat, NULL, &hdr, data, payload_size);
1699: 		return GF_OK;
1700: 	}
1701: 
1702: 	if (paf && paf->PCR_flag) {
1703: 		if (!es) {
1704: 			u32 i, j;
1705: 			for(i=0; i<gf_list_count(ts->programs); i++) {
1706: 				GF_M2TS_PES *first_pes = NULL;
1707: 				GF_M2TS_Program *program = (GF_M2TS_Program *)gf_list_get(ts->programs,i);
1708: 				if(program->pcr_pid != hdr.pid) continue;
1709: 				for (j=0; j<gf_list_count(program->streams); j++) {
1710: 					GF_M2TS_PES *pes = (GF_M2TS_PES *) gf_list_get(program->streams, j);
1711: 					if (pes->flags & GF_M2TS_INHERIT_PCR) {
1712: 						ts->ess[hdr.pid] = (GF_M2TS_ES *) pes;
1713: 						pes->flags |= GF_M2TS_FAKE_PCR | GF_M2TS_ES_IS_PCR_REUSE;
1714: 						break;
1715: 					}
1716: 					if (pes->flags & GF_M2TS_ES_IS_PES) {
1717: 						first_pes = pes;
1718: 					}
1719: 				}
1720: 				//non found, use the first media stream as a PCR destination - Q: is it legal to have PCR only streams not declared in PMT ?
1721: 				if (!es && first_pes) {
1722: 					es = (GF_M2TS_ES *) first_pes;
1723: 					first_pes->flags |= GF_M2TS_FAKE_PCR;
1724: 				}
1725: 				break;
1726: 			}
1727: 			if (!es)
1728: 				es = ts->ess[hdr.pid];
1729: 		}
1730: 		if (es) {
1731: 			GF_M2TS_PES_PCK pck;
1732: 			s64 prev_diff_in_us;
1733: 			Bool discontinuity;
1734: 			s32 cc = -1;
1735: 
1736: 			if (es->flags & GF_M2TS_FAKE_PCR) {
1737: 				cc = es->program->pcr_cc;
1738: 				es->program->pcr_cc = hdr.continuity_counter;
1739: 			}
1740: 			else if (es->flags & GF_M2TS_ES_IS_PES) cc = ((GF_M2TS_PES*)es)->cc;
1741: 			else if (((GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES*)es)->sec) cc = ((GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES*)es)->sec->cc;
1742: 
1743: 			discontinuity = paf->discontinuity_indicator;
1744: 			if ((cc>=0) && es->program->before_last_pcr_value) {
1745: 				//no increment of CC if AF only packet
1746: 				if (hdr.adaptation_field == 2) {
1747: 					if (hdr.continuity_counter != cc) {
1748: 						discontinuity = GF_TRUE;
1749: 					}
1750: 				} else if (hdr.continuity_counter != ((cc + 1) & 0xF)) {
1751: 					discontinuity = GF_TRUE;
1752: 				}
1753: 			}
1754: 
1755: 			memset(&pck, 0, sizeof(GF_M2TS_PES_PCK));
1756: 			prev_diff_in_us = (s64) (es->program->last_pcr_value /27- es->program->before_last_pcr_value/27);
1757: 			es->program->before_last_pcr_value = es->program->last_pcr_value;
1758: 			es->program->before_last_pcr_value_pck_number = es->program->last_pcr_value_pck_number;
1759: 			es->program->last_pcr_value_pck_number = ts->pck_number;
1760: 			es->program->last_pcr_value = paf->PCR_base * 300 + paf->PCR_ext;
1761: 			if (!es->program->last_pcr_value) es->program->last_pcr_value =  1;
1762: 
1763: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] PID %d PCR found "LLU" ("LLU" at 90kHz) - PCR diff is %d us\n", hdr.pid, es->program->last_pcr_value, es->program->last_pcr_value/300, (s32) (es->program->last_pcr_value - es->program->before_last_pcr_value)/27 ));
1764: 
1765: 			pck.PTS = es->program->last_pcr_value;
1766: 			pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_PES *)es;
1767: 
1768: 			//try to ignore all discontinuities that are less than 200 ms (seen in some HLS setup ...)
1769: 			if (discontinuity) {
1770: 				s64 diff_in_us = (s64) (es->program->last_pcr_value - es->program->before_last_pcr_value) / 27;
1771: 				u64 diff = ABS(diff_in_us - prev_diff_in_us);
1772: 
1773: 				if ((diff_in_us<0) && (diff_in_us >= -200000)) {
1774: 					GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] PID %d new PCR, with discontinuity signaled, is less than previously received PCR (diff %d us) but not too large, trying to ignore discontinuity\n", hdr.pid, diff_in_us));
1775: 				}
1776: 
1777: 				//ignore PCR discontinuity indicator if PCR found is larger than previously received PCR and diffence between PCR before and after discontinuity indicator is smaller than 50ms
1778: 				else if ((diff_in_us > 0) && (diff < 200000)) {
1779: 					GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] PID %d PCR discontinuity signaled but diff is small (diff %d us - PCR diff %d vs prev PCR diff %d) - ignore it\n", hdr.pid, diff, diff_in_us, prev_diff_in_us));
1780: 				} else if (paf->discontinuity_indicator) {
1781: 					GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] PID %d PCR discontinuity signaled (diff %d us - PCR diff %d vs prev PCR diff %d)\n", hdr.pid, diff, diff_in_us, prev_diff_in_us));
1782: 					pck.flags = GF_M2TS_PES_PCK_DISCONTINUITY;
1783: 				} else {
1784: 					GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] PID %d PCR discontinuity not signaled (diff %d us - PCR diff %d vs prev PCR diff %d)\n", hdr.pid, diff, diff_in_us, prev_diff_in_us));
1785: 					pck.flags = GF_M2TS_PES_PCK_DISCONTINUITY;
1786: 				}
1787: 			}
1788: 			else if ((es->flags & GF_M2TS_CHECK_DISC) && (es->program->last_pcr_value < es->program->before_last_pcr_value) ) {
1789: 				s64 diff_in_us = (s64) (es->program->last_pcr_value - es->program->before_last_pcr_value) / 27;
1790: 				//if less than 200 ms before PCR loop at the last PCR, this is a PCR loop
1791: 				if (GF_M2TS_MAX_PCR - es->program->before_last_pcr_value < 5400000 /*2*2700000*/) {
1792: 					GF_LOG(GF_LOG_INFO, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] PID %d PCR loop found from "LLU" to "LLU" \n", hdr.pid, es->program->before_last_pcr_value, es->program->last_pcr_value));
1793: 				} else if ((diff_in_us<0) && (diff_in_us >= -200000)) {
1794: 					GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] PID %d new PCR, without discontinuity signaled, is less than previously received PCR (diff %d us) but not too large, trying to ignore discontinuity\n", hdr.pid, diff_in_us));
1795: 				} else {
1796: 					GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] PID %d PCR found "LLU" is less than previously received PCR "LLU" (PCR diff %g sec) but no discontinuity signaled\n", hdr.pid, es->program->last_pcr_value, es->program->before_last_pcr_value, (GF_M2TS_MAX_PCR - es->program->before_last_pcr_value + es->program->last_pcr_value) / 27000000.0));
1797: 
1798: 					pck.flags = GF_M2TS_PES_PCK_DISCONTINUITY;
1799: 				}
1800: 			}
1801: 
1802: 			if (pck.flags & GF_M2TS_PES_PCK_DISCONTINUITY) {
1803: 				gf_m2ts_reset_parsers_for_program(ts, es->program);
1804: 			}
1805: 
1806: 			if (ts->on_event) {
1807: 				ts->on_event(ts, GF_M2TS_EVT_PES_PCR, &pck);
1808: 			}
1809: 		}
1810: 	}
1811: 
1812: 	/*check for DVB reserved PIDs*/
1813: 	if (!es) {
1814: 		if (hdr.pid == GF_M2TS_PID_SDT_BAT_ST) {
1815: 			gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ts->sdt, NULL, &hdr, data, payload_size);
1816: 			return GF_OK;
1817: 		} else if (hdr.pid == GF_M2TS_PID_NIT_ST) {
1818: 			/*ignore them, unused at application level*/
1819: 			gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ts->nit, NULL, &hdr, data, payload_size);
1820: 			return GF_OK;
1821: 		} else if (hdr.pid == GF_M2TS_PID_EIT_ST_CIT) {
1822: 			/* ignore EIT messages for the moment */
1823: 			gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ts->eit, NULL, &hdr, data, payload_size);
1824: 			return GF_OK;
1825: 		} else if (hdr.pid == GF_M2TS_PID_TDT_TOT_ST) {
1826: 			gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ts->tdt_tot, NULL, &hdr, data, payload_size);
1827: 		} else {
1828: 			/* ignore packet */
1829: 		}
1830: 	} else if (es->flags & GF_M2TS_ES_IS_SECTION) { 	/* The stream uses sections to carry its payload */
1831: 		GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *ses = (GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *)es;
1832: 		if (ses->sec) gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ses->sec, ses, &hdr, data, payload_size);
1833: 	} else {
1834: 		GF_M2TS_PES *pes = (GF_M2TS_PES *)es;
1835: 		/* regular stream using PES packets */
1836: 		if (pes->reframe && payload_size) gf_m2ts_process_pes(ts, pes, &hdr, data, payload_size, paf);
1837: 	}
1838: 
1839: 	return GF_OK;
1840: }
1841: 
1842: GF_EXPORT
1843: GF_Err gf_m2ts_process_data(GF_M2TS_Demuxer *ts, u8 *data, u32 data_size)
1844: {
1845: 	GF_Err e=GF_OK;
1846: 	u32 pos, pck_size;
1847: 	Bool is_align = 1;
1848: 
1849: 	if (ts->buffer_size) {

978: static void m2tsdmx_on_event(GF_M2TS_Demuxer *ts, u32 evt_type, void *param)
979: {
980: 	u32 i, count;
981: 	GF_Filter *filter = (GF_Filter *) ts->user;
982: 	GF_M2TSDmxCtx *ctx = gf_filter_get_udta(filter);
983: 
984: 	switch (evt_type) {
985: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_PAT_UPDATE:
986: 		break;
987: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_AIT_FOUND:
988: 		break;
989: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_PAT_FOUND:
990: 		if (ctx->mux_tune_state==DMX_TUNE_INIT) {
991: 			ctx->mux_tune_state = DMX_TUNE_WAIT_PROGS;
992: 			ctx->wait_for_progs = gf_list_count(ts->programs);
993: 		}
994: 		break;
995: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_DSMCC_FOUND:
996: 		break;
997: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_PMT_FOUND:
998: 		m2tsdmx_setup_program(ctx, param);
999: 		if (ctx->mux_tune_state == DMX_TUNE_WAIT_PROGS) {
1000: 			gf_fatal_assert(ctx->wait_for_progs);
1001: 			ctx->wait_for_progs--;
1002: 			if (!ctx->wait_for_progs) {
1003: 				ctx->mux_tune_state = DMX_TUNE_WAIT_SEEK;
1004: 			}
1005: 		}
1006: 		break;
1007: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_PMT_REPEAT:
1008: 		break;
1009: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_PMT_UPDATE:
1010: 		m2tsdmx_setup_program(ctx, param);
1011: 		break;
1012: 
1013: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_SDT_FOUND:
1014: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_SDT_UPDATE:
1015: 		m2tsdmx_update_sdt(ts, NULL);
1016: 		break;
1017: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_DVB_GENERAL:
1018: 		if (ctx->eit_pid) {
1019: 			GF_M2TS_SL_PCK *pck = (GF_M2TS_SL_PCK *)param;
1020: 			u8 *data;
1021: 			GF_FilterPacket *dst_pck = gf_filter_pck_new_alloc(ctx->eit_pid, pck->data_len, &data);
1022: 			if (dst_pck) {
1023: 				memcpy(data, pck->data, pck->data_len);
1024: 				gf_filter_pck_send(dst_pck);
1025: 			}
1026: 		}
1027: 		break;
1028: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_PES_PCK:
1029: 		if (ctx->mux_tune_state) break;
1030: 		m2tsdmx_send_packet(ctx, param);
1031: 		break;
1032: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_SL_PCK:
1033: 		if (ctx->mux_tune_state) break;
1034: 		m2tsdmx_send_sl_packet(ctx, param);
1035: 		break;
1036: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_PES_PCR:
1037: 		if (ctx->mux_tune_state) break;
1038: 	{
1039: 		u64 pcr;
1040: 		Bool map_time = GF_FALSE;
1041: 		GF_M2TS_PES_PCK *pck = ((GF_M2TS_PES_PCK *) param);
1042: 		Bool discontinuity = ( ((GF_M2TS_PES_PCK *) param)->flags & GF_M2TS_PES_PCK_DISCONTINUITY) ? 1 : 0;
1043: 
1044: 		gf_fatal_assert(pck->stream);
1045: 		if (!ctx->sigfrag && ctx->index) {
1046: 			m2tsdmx_estimate_duration(ctx, (GF_M2TS_ES *) pck->stream);
1047: 		}
1048: 
1049: 		if (ctx->map_time_on_prog_id && (ctx->map_time_on_prog_id==pck->stream->program->number)) {
1050: 			map_time = GF_TRUE;
1051: 		}
1052: 
1053: 		pcr = ((GF_M2TS_PES_PCK *) param)->PTS;
1054: 		pcr /= 300;
1055: 		count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);
1056: 		for (i=0; i<count; i++) {
1057: 			GF_FilterPacket *dst_pck;
1058: 			GF_M2TS_PES *stream = gf_list_get(pck->stream->program->streams, i);
1059: 			if (!stream->user) continue;
1060: 
1061: 			dst_pck = gf_filter_pck_new_shared(stream->user, NULL, 0, NULL);
1062: 			if (!dst_pck) continue;
1063: 
1064: 			gf_filter_pck_set_cts(dst_pck, pcr);
1065: 			gf_filter_pck_set_clock_type(dst_pck, discontinuity ? GF_FILTER_CLOCK_PCR_DISC : GF_FILTER_CLOCK_PCR);
1066: 			if (pck->stream->is_seg_start) {
1067: 				pck->stream->is_seg_start = GF_FALSE;
1068: 				gf_filter_pck_set_property(dst_pck, GF_PROP_PCK_CUE_START, &PROP_BOOL(GF_TRUE));
1069: 			}
1070: 			gf_filter_pck_send(dst_pck);
1071: 
1072: 			if (map_time && (stream->flags & GF_M2TS_ES_IS_PES) ) {
1073: 				((GF_M2TS_PES*)stream)->map_pcr = pcr;
1074: 			}
1075: 		}
1076: 
1077: 		if (map_time) {
1078: 			ctx->map_time_on_prog_id = 0;
1079: 		}
1080: 	}
1081: 		break;
1082: 
1083: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_TDT:
1084: 		if (ctx->mux_tune_state) break;
1085: 	{
1086: 		GF_M2TS_TDT_TOT *tdt = (GF_M2TS_TDT_TOT *)param;
1087: 		u64 utc_ts = gf_net_get_utc_ts(tdt->year, tdt->month, tdt->day, tdt->hour, tdt->minute, tdt->second);
1088: 		count = gf_list_count(ts->programs );
1089: 		for (i=0; i<count; i++) {
1090: 			GF_M2TS_Program *prog = gf_list_get(ts->programs, i);
1091: 			u32 j, count2 = gf_list_count(prog->streams);
1092: 			for (j=0; j<count2; j++) {
1093: 				GF_M2TS_ES * stream = gf_list_get(prog->streams, j);
1094: 				if (stream->user && (stream->flags & GF_M2TS_ES_IS_PES)) {
1095: 					GF_M2TS_PES*pes = (GF_M2TS_PES*)stream;
1096: 					pes->map_utc = utc_ts;
1097: 					pes->map_utc_pcr = prog->last_pcr_value/300;
1098: 				}
1099: 			}
1100: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[M2TS In] Mapping TDT Time %04d-%02d-%02dT%02d:%02d:%02d and PCR time "LLD" on program %d\n",
1101: 				                                       tdt->year, tdt->month+1, tdt->day, tdt->hour, tdt->minute, tdt->second, prog->last_pcr_value/300, prog->number));
1102: 		}
1103: 	}
1104: 		break;
1105: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_TOT:
1106: 		break;
1107: 
1108: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_DURATION_ESTIMATED:
1109: 	{
1110: 		u64 duration = ((GF_M2TS_PES_PCK *) param)->PTS;
1111: 		count = gf_list_count(ts->programs);
1112: 		for (i=0; i<count; i++) {
1113: 			GF_M2TS_Program *prog = gf_list_get(ts->programs, i);
1114: 			u32 j, count2;
1115: 			count2 = gf_list_count(prog->streams);
1116: 			for (j=0; j<count2; j++) {
1117: 				GF_M2TS_ES * stream = gf_list_get(prog->streams, j);
1118: 				if (stream->user) {
1119: 					gf_filter_pid_set_property(stream->user, GF_PROP_PID_DURATION, & PROP_FRAC64_INT(duration, 1000) );
1120: 				}
1121: 			}
1122: 		}
1123: 	}
1124: 	break;
1125: 
1126: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_TEMI_LOCATION:
1127: 	{
1128: 		GF_M2TS_TemiLocationDescriptor *temi_l = (GF_M2TS_TemiLocationDescriptor *)param;
1129: 		const char *url;
1130: 		u32 len;
1131: 		void *bs;
1132: 		GF_M2TS_ES *es=NULL;
1133: 		GF_M2TS_Prop_TEMIInfo *t;
1134: 		if ((temi_l->pid<8192) && (ctx->ts->ess[temi_l->pid])) {
1135: 			es = ctx->ts->ess[temi_l->pid];
1136: 		}
1137: 		if (!es || !es->user) {
1138: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[M2TSDmx] TEMI location not assigned to a given PID, not supported\n"));
1139: 			break;
1140: 		}
1141: 		GF_SAFEALLOC(t, GF_M2TS_Prop_TEMIInfo);
1142: 		if (!t) break;
1143: 		t->timeline_id = temi_l->timeline_id;
1144: 		t->is_loc = GF_TRUE;
1145: 
1146: 		bs = NULL;
1147: 		if (ctx->temi_url)
1148: 			url = ctx->temi_url;
1149: 		else
1150: 			url = temi_l->external_URL;
1151: 		len = url ? (u32) strlen(url) : 0;
1152: 		t->data = (u8*)gf_malloc(len + 1);
1153: 		if (url) memcpy(t->data, url, len);
1154: 		t->data[len] = 0;
1155: 		t->len = len + 4;
1156: 
1157: 		if (!es->props) {
1158: 			es->props = gf_list_new();
1159: 		}
1160: 		gf_list_add(es->props, t);
1161: 	}
1162: 	break;
1163: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_TEMI_TIMECODE:
1164: 	{
1165: 		GF_M2TS_TemiTimecodeDescriptor *temi_t = (GF_M2TS_TemiTimecodeDescriptor*)param;
1166: 		void *bs;
1167: 		GF_M2TS_Prop_TEMIInfo *t;
1168: 		GF_M2TS_ES *es=NULL;
1169: 		if ((temi_t->pid<8192) && (ctx->ts->ess[temi_t->pid])) {
1170: 			es = ctx->ts->ess[temi_t->pid];
1171: 		}
1172: 		if (!es || !es->user) {
1173: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[M2TSDmx] TEMI timing not assigned to a given PID, not supported\n"));
1174: 			break;
1175: 		}
1176: 		GF_SAFEALLOC(t, GF_M2TS_Prop_TEMIInfo);
1177: 		if (!t) break;
1178: 		t->type = M2TS_TEMI_INFO;
1179: 		t->timeline_id = temi_t->timeline_id;
1180: 
1181: 		t->data = (u8*)gf_malloc(25);
1182: 		memset(t->data, 0, 25);
1183: 		t->len = 25;
1184: 
1185: 		if (!es->props) {
1186: 			es->props = gf_list_new();
1187: 		}
1188: 		gf_list_add(es->props, t);
1189: 	}
1190: 	break;
1191: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_ID3:
1192: 	{
1193: 		GF_M2TS_PES_PCK *pck = (GF_M2TS_PES_PCK*)param;
1194: 		void *bs;
1195: 		GF_M2TS_Prop *t;
1196: 		if (!pck->stream) return;
1197: 		u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);
1198: 		for (i=0; i<count; i++) {
1199: 			GF_M2TS_PES *es = gf_list_get(pck->stream->program->streams, i);
1200: 			if (!es->user) {
1201: 				GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[M2TSDmx] ID3 metadata not assigned to a given PID, not supported\n"));
1202: 				continue;
1203: 			}
1204: 
1205: 			GF_FilterPid *opid = (GF_FilterPid *)es->user;
1206: 			const GF_PropertyValue *p = gf_filter_pid_get_property(opid, GF_PROP_PID_STREAM_TYPE);
1207: 			if (!p) return;
1208: 			if (p->value.uint != GF_STREAM_AUDIO)
1209: 				continue;
1210: 
1211: 			GF_SAFEALLOC(t, GF_M2TS_Prop);
1212: 			if (!t) break;
1213: 			t->type = M2TS_ID3;
1214: 			t->data = (u8*)gf_malloc(pck->data_len + 12);
1215: 			memcpy(t->data, &pck->PTS, 8);
1216: 			memcpy(t->data + 8, &pck->data_len, 4);
1217: 			memcpy(t->data + 12, pck->data, pck->data_len);
1218: 			t->len = pck->data_len + 12;
1219: 
1220: 			if (!es->props) {
1221: 				es->props = gf_list_new();
1222: 			}
1223: 			gf_list_add(es->props, t);
1224: 		}
1225: 	}
1226: 	break;
1227: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_SCTE35_SPLICE_INFO:
1228: 	{
1229: 		GF_M2TS_SL_PCK *pck = (GF_M2TS_SL_PCK*)param;
1230: 		void *bs;
1231: 		GF_M2TS_Prop *t;
1232: 
1233: 		u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);
1234: 		for (i=0; i<count; i++) {
1235: 			GF_M2TS_PES *es = gf_list_get(pck->stream->program->streams, i);
1236: 			if (!es->user) {
1237: 				GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[M2TSDmx] SCTE35 section not assigned to a given PID, not supported\n"));
1238: 				continue;
1239: 			}
1240: 
1241: 			GF_FilterPid *opid = (GF_FilterPid *)es->user;
1242: 			const GF_PropertyValue *p = gf_filter_pid_get_property(opid, GF_PROP_PID_STREAM_TYPE);
1243: 			if (!p) return;
1244: 			if (p->value.uint != GF_STREAM_VISUAL)
1245: 				continue;
1246: 
1247: 			GF_SAFEALLOC(t, GF_M2TS_Prop);
1248: 			if (!t) break;
1249: 			t->type = M2TS_SCTE35;
1250: 			t->data = (u8*)gf_malloc(pck->data_len);
1251: 			memcpy(t->data, pck->data, pck->data_len);
1252: 			t->len = pck->data_len;
1253: 
1254: 			if (!es->props) {
1255: 				es->props = gf_list_new();
1256: 			}
1257: 			gf_list_add(es->props, t);
1258: 
1259: 			break;
1260: 		}
1261: 	}
1262: 	break;
1263: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_STREAM_REMOVED:
1264: 	{
1265: 		GF_M2TS_ES *es = (GF_M2TS_ES *)param;
1266: 		if (es && es->props) {
1267: 			while (gf_list_count(es->props)) {
1268: 				GF_M2TS_Prop *t = gf_list_pop_back(es->props);
1269: 				gf_free(t->data);
1270: 				gf_free(t);
1271: 			}
1272: 			gf_list_del(es->props);
1273: 		}
1274: 	}
1275: 		break;
1276: 	}
1277: }

1279: static void gf_m2ts_section_complete(GF_M2TS_Demuxer *ts, GF_M2TS_SectionFilter *sec, GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *ses)
1280: {
1281: 	if (ts->seek_mode && (sec->section[0] != 0x00) && (sec->section[0] != 0x02)) {
1282: 		if (sec->section) gf_free(sec->section);
1283: 		sec->section = NULL;
1284: 		sec->length = sec->received = 0;
1285: 		return;
1286: 	}
1287: 
1288: 	if (!sec->process_section) {
1289: 		if ((ts->on_event && (sec->section[0]==0x74)) ) {
1290: 			GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
1291: 			pck.data_len = sec->length;
1292: 			pck.data = sec->section;
1293: 			pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
1294: 		} else if ((ts->on_event && (sec->section[0]==0x3A || sec->section[0]==0x3B ||
1295: 		                             sec->section[0]==0x3C || sec->section[0]==0x3D || sec->section[0]==0x3E)) ) {
1296: 
1297: 			GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
1298: 			pck.data_len = sec->length;
1299: 			pck.data = sec->section;
1300: 			pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
1301: 		}
1302: 		else if ((ts->on_event && (sec->section[0]==0xFC)) ) {
1303: 			GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
1304: 			pck.data_len = sec->length;
1305: 			pck.data = sec->section;
1306: 			pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
1307: 			ts->on_event(ts, 30, &pck);
1308: 		}
1309: 		else if (ts->on_event) {
1310: 			GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
1311: 			pck.data_len = sec->length;
1312: 			pck.data = sec->section;
1313: 			pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
1314: 			ts->on_event(ts, 24, &pck);
1315: 		}
1316: 	} else {
1317: 		Bool has_syntax_indicator;
1318: 		u8 table_id;
1319: 		u16 extended_table_id;
1320: 		u32 status, section_start, i;
1321: 		GF_M2TS_Table *t, *prev_t;
1322: 		unsigned char *data;
1323: 		Bool section_valid = 0;
1324: 
1325: 		status = 0;
1326: 		data = (u8 *)sec->section;
1327: 
1328: 		if (sec->length < 2) {
1329: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] M2TS Table parsing error, length %d is too small\n", sec->length) );
1330: 			return;
1331: 		}
1332: 
1333: 		table_id = data[0];
1334: 
1335: 		if (ts->on_event) {
1336: 			switch (table_id) {
1337: 			case 0x00:
1338: 			case 0x42:
1339: 			case 0x02:
1340: 			case 0x40:
1341: 			case 0x70:
1342: 			case 0x73:
1343: 			{
1344: 				GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
1345: 				pck.data_len = sec->length;
1346: 				pck.data = sec->section;
1347: 				pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
1348: 				ts->on_event(ts, 24, &pck);
1349: 			}
1350: 			}
1351: 		}
1352: 
1353: 		has_syntax_indicator = (data[1] & 0x80) ? 1 : 0;
1354: 		if (has_syntax_indicator) {
1355: 			if (sec->length < 5) {
1356: 				GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] M2TS Table parsing error, length %d is too small\n", sec->length) );
1357: 				return;
1358: 			}
1359: 			extended_table_id = (data[3]<<8) | data[4];
1360: 		} else {
1361: 			extended_table_id = 0;
1362: 		}
1363: 
1364: 		prev_t = NULL;
1365: 		t = sec->table;
1366: 		while (t) {
1367: 			if ((t->table_id==table_id) && (t->ex_table_id == extended_table_id)) break;
1368: 			prev_t = t;
1369: 			t = t->next;
1370: 		}
1371: 
1372: 		if (!t) {
1373: 			GF_SAFEALLOC(t, GF_M2TS_Table);
1374: 			if (!t) {
1375: 				GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] Fail to alloc table %d %d\n", table_id, extended_table_id));
1376: 				return;
1377: 			}
1378: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] Creating table %d %d\n", table_id, extended_table_id));
1379: 			t->table_id = table_id;
1380: 			t->ex_table_id = extended_table_id;
1381: 			t->last_version_number = 0xFF;
1382: 			t->sections = gf_list_new();
1383: 			if (prev_t) prev_t->next = t;
1384: 			else sec->table = t;
1385: 		}
1386: 
1387: 		if (has_syntax_indicator) {
1388: 			if (sec->length < 4) {
1389: 				GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] corrupted section length %d less than CRC \n", sec->length));
1390: 			} else {
1391: 				sec->length -= 4;
1392: 				if (1) {
1393: 					s32 cur_sec_num;
1394: 					t->version_number = (data[5] >> 1) & 0x1f;
1395: 					if (t->last_section_number && t->section_number && (t->version_number != t->last_version_number)) {
1396: 						GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] table transmission interrupted: previous table (v=%d) %d/%d sections - new table (v=%d) %d/%d sections\n", t->last_version_number, t->section_number, t->last_section_number, t->version_number, data[6] + 1, data[7] + 1) );
1397: 						t->section_number = 0;
1398: 					}
1399: 
1400: 					t->current_next_indicator = (data[5] & 0x1) ? 1 : 0;
1401: 					cur_sec_num = data[6] + 1;
1402: 					t->last_section_number = data[7] + 1;
1403: 					section_start = 8;
1404: 					if (!sec->process_individual && t->section_number + 1 != cur_sec_num) {
1405: 						section_valid = 0;
1406: 						if (t->is_init) {
1407: 							GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] corrupted table (lost section %d)\n", cur_sec_num ? cur_sec_num-1 : 31) );
1408: 						}
1409: 					} else {
1410: 						section_valid = 1;
1411: 						t->section_number = cur_sec_num;
1412: 					}
1413: 				} else {
1414: 					GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] corrupted section (CRC32 failed)\n"));
1415: 				}
1416: 			}
1417: 		} else {
1418: 			section_valid = 1;
1419: 			section_start = 3;
1420: 		}
1421: 		if (section_valid) {
1422: 			GF_M2TS_Section *section;
1423: 
1424: 			GF_SAFEALLOC(section, GF_M2TS_Section);
1425: 			if (!section) {
1426: 				GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] Fail to create section\n"));
1427: 				return;
1428: 			}
1429: 			section->data_size = sec->length - section_start;
1430: 			section->data = (unsigned char*)gf_malloc(sizeof(unsigned char)*section->data_size);
1431: 			memcpy(section->data, sec->section + section_start, sizeof(unsigned char)*section->data_size);
1432: 			gf_list_add(t->sections, section);
1433: 
1434: 			if (t->section_number == 1) {
1435: 				status |= 1;
1436: 				if (t->last_version_number == t->version_number) {
1437: 					t->is_repeat = 1;
1438: 				} else {
1439: 					t->is_repeat = 0;
1440: 				}
1441: 				t->last_version_number = t->version_number;
1442: 			}
1443: 
1444: 			if (t->is_init) {
1445: 				if (t->is_repeat) {
1446: 					status |=  1<<3;
1447: 				} else {
1448: 					status |=  1<<2;
1449: 				}
1450: 			} else {
1451: 				status |=  1<<1;
1452: 			}
1453: 
1454: 			if (t->last_section_number == t->section_number) {
1455: 				u32 table_size;
1456: 
1457: 				status |= 1<<0;
1458: 
1459: 				table_size = 0;
1460: 				for (i=0; i<gf_list_count(t->sections); i++) {
1461: 					GF_M2TS_Section *a_sec = gf_list_get(t->sections, i);
1462: 					table_size += a_sec->data_size;
1463: 				}
1464: 				if (t->is_repeat) {
1465: 					if (t->table_size != table_size) {
1466: 						status |= 1<<2;
1467: 						GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] Repeated section found with different sizes (old table %d bytes, new table %d bytes)\n", t->table_size, table_size) );
1468: 
1469: 						t->table_size = table_size;
1470: 					}
1471: 				} else {
1472: 					t->table_size = table_size;
1473: 				}
1474: 
1475: 				t->is_init = 1;
1476: 				t->section_number = 0;
1477: 
1478: 				t->is_repeat = 0;
1479: 
1480: 			}
1481: 
1482: 			if (sec->process_individual) {
1483: 				if (sec->process_section)
1484: 					sec->process_section(ts, ses, t->sections, t->table_id, t->ex_table_id, t->version_number, (u8) (t->last_section_number - 1), status);
1485: 
1486: 				t->section_number = 0;
1487: 			} else {
1488: 				if (status& (1<<0)) {
1489: 					if (sec->process_section)
1490: 						sec->process_section(ts, ses, t->sections, t->table_id, t->ex_table_id, t->version_number, (u8) (t->last_section_number - 1), status);
1491: 
1492: 					t->section_number = 0;
1493: 				}
1494: 			}
1495: 
1496: 		} else {
1497: 			sec->cc = -1;
1498: 			t->section_number = 0;
1499: 		}
1500: 	}
1501: 	if (sec->section) gf_free(sec->section);
1502: 	sec->section = NULL;
1503: 	sec->length = sec->received = 0;
1504: }

1506: static void gf_m2ts_gather_section(GF_M2TS_Demuxer *ts, GF_M2TS_SectionFilter *sec, GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *ses, void *hdr, unsigned char *data, u32 data_size)
1507: {
1508: 	u32 payload_size = data_size;
1509: 	u8 expect_cc = (sec->cc<0) ? ((u8*)hdr)[3] & 0xf : (sec->cc + 1) & 0xf;
1510: 	Bool disc = (expect_cc == ((u8*)hdr)[3] & 0xf) ? 0 : 1;
1511: 	sec->cc = expect_cc;
1512: 
1513: 	if (!data_size) return;

1558: 		return;
1559: 	} else if (!sec->section) {
1560: 		return;
1561: 	} else {
1562: 		if (sec->length && sec->received+data_size > sec->length)

1564:

1566: 			memcpy(sec->section + sec->received, data, sizeof(char)*data_size);

1577: 		if (sec->received > sec->length) {
1578: 			data_size -= sec->received - sec->length;
1579: 			sec->received = sec->length;
1580: 		}
1581: 	}
1582: 	if (!sec->length || sec->received < sec->length) return;

1596: static GF_Err gf_m2ts_process_packet(GF_M2TS_Demuxer *ts, unsigned char *data)
1597: {
1598: 	GF_M2TS_ES *es;
1599: 	GF_M2TS_Header hdr;
1600: 	GF_M2TS_AdaptationField af, *paf;
1601: 	u32 payload_size, af_size;
1602: 	u32 pos = 0;
1603: 
1604: 	ts->pck_number++;
1605: 
1606: 	/* read TS packet header*/
1607: 	hdr.sync = data[0];
1608: 	if (hdr.sync != 0x47) {
1609: 		GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d does not start with sync marker\n", ts->pck_number));
1610: 		return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1611: 	}
1612: 	hdr.error = (data[1] & 0x80) ? 1 : 0;
1613: 	hdr.payload_start = (data[1] & 0x40) ? 1 : 0;
1614: 	hdr.priority = (data[1] & 0x20) ? 1 : 0;
1615: 	hdr.pid = ( (data[1]&0x1f) << 8) | data[2];
1616: 	hdr.scrambling_ctrl = (data[3] >> 6) & 0x3;
1617: 	hdr.adaptation_field = (data[3] >> 4) & 0x3;
1618: 	hdr.continuity_counter = data[3] & 0xf;

1620: 	if (hdr.error) {
1621: 		GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d has error (PID could be %d)\n", ts->pck_number, hdr.pid));
1622: 		return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1623: 	}
1624: //#if DEBUG_TS_PACKET
1625: 	GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d PID %d CC %d Encrypted %d\n", ts->pck_number, hdr.pid, hdr.continuity_counter, hdr.scrambling_ctrl));
1626: //#endif
1627: 
1628: 	if (hdr.scrambling_ctrl) {
1629: 		//TODO add decyphering
1630: 		GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d is scrambled - not supported\n", ts->pck_number, hdr.pid));
1631: 		return GF_NOT_SUPPORTED;
1632: 	}
1633: 
1634: 	paf = NULL;
1635: 	payload_size = 184;
1636: 	pos = 4;
1637: 	switch (hdr.adaptation_field) {
1638: 	/*adaptation+data*/
1639: 	case 3:
1640: 		af_size = data[4];
1641: 		if (af_size>183) {
1642: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_ERROR, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d AF field larger than 183  for AF type 3!\n", ts->pck_number));
1643: 			//error
1644: 			return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1645: 		}
1646: 		paf = ⁡
1647: 		memset(paf, 0, sizeof(GF_M2TS_AdaptationField));
1648: 		if (af_size) gf_m2ts_get_adaptation_field(ts, paf, data+5, af_size, hdr.pid);
1649: 		pos += 1+af_size;
1650: 		payload_size = 183 - af_size;
1651: 		break;
1652: 	/*adaptation only - still process in case of PCR*/
1653: 	case 2:
1654: 		af_size = data[4];
1655: 		if (af_size != 183) {
1656: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_WARNING, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] TS Packet %d AF size is %d when it must be 183 for AF type 2\n", ts->pck_number, af_size));
1657: 			return GF_CORRUPTED_DATA;
1658: 		}
1659: 		paf = ⁡
1660: 		memset(paf, 0, sizeof(GF_M2TS_AdaptationField));
1661: 		gf_m2ts_get_adaptation_field(ts, paf, data+5, af_size, hdr.pid);
1662: 		payload_size = 0;
1663: 		/*no payload and no PCR, return*/
1664: 		if (!paf->PCR_flag)
1665: 			return GF_OK;
1666: 		break;
1667: 	/*reserved*/
1668: 	case 0:
1669: 		return GF_OK;
1670: 	default:
1671: 		break;
1672: 	}
1673: 	data += pos;
1674: 
1675: 	/*PAT*/
1676: 	if (hdr.pid == GF_M2TS_PID_PAT) {
1677: 		gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ts->pat, NULL, &hdr, data, payload_size);
1678: 		return GF_OK;
1679: 	}
1680: 
1681: 	es = ts->ess[hdr.pid];
1682: 	//we work in split mode
1683: 	if (ts->split_mode) {

1685: 		//process PMT table
1686: 		if (es && (es->flags & GF_M2TS_ES_IS_PMT)) {
1687: 			GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *ses = (GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *)es;
1688: 			if (ses->sec) gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ses->sec, ses, &hdr, data, payload_size);

1690: 		//and forward every packet other than PAT
1691: 		tspck.stream = es;
1692: 		tspck.pid = hdr.pid;
1693: 		tspck.data = data - pos;

1697: 
1698: 	if (hdr.pid == GF_M2TS_PID_CAT) {

1702: 
1703: 	if (paf && paf->PCR_flag) {

1727: 			}
1728: 			if (!es)
1729: 				es = ts->ess[hdr.pid];
1730: 		}
1731: 		if (es) {
1732: 			GF_M2TS_PES_PCK pck;
1733: 			s64 prev_diff_in_us;
1734: 			Bool discontinuity;
1735: 			s32 cc = -1;
1736: 
1737: 			if (es->flags & GF_M2TS_FAKE_PCR) {
1738: 				cc = es->program->pcr_cc;
1739: 				es->program->pcr_cc = hdr.continuity_counter;

1741: 			else if (es->flags & GF_M2TS_ES_IS_PES) cc = ((GF_M2TS_PES*)es)->cc;
1742: 			else if (((GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES*)es)->sec) cc = ((GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES*)es)->sec->cc;

1744: 			discontinuity = paf->discontinuity_indicator;
1745: 			if ((cc>=0) && es->program->before_last_pcr_value) {

1747: 				if (hdr.adaptation_field == 2) {

1750: 					}
1751: 				} else if (hdr.continuity_counter != ((cc + 1) & 0xF)) {

1753: 				}
1754: 			}
1755: 
1756: 			memset(&pck, 0, sizeof(GF_M2TS_PES_PCK));
1757: 			prev_diff_in_us = (s64) (es->program->last_pcr_value /27- es->program->before_last_pcr_value/27);
1758: 			es->program->before_last_pcr_value = es->program->last_pcr_value;
1759: 			es->program->before_last_pcr_value_pck_number = es->program->last_pcr_value_pck_number;

1764: 			GF_LOG(GF_LOG_DEBUG, GF_LOG_CONTAINER, ("[MPEG-2 TS] PID %d PCR found "LLU" ("LLU" at 90kHz) - PCR diff is %d us\n", hdr.pid, es->program->last_pcr_value, es->program->last_pcr_value/300, (s32) (es->program->last_pcr_value - es->program->before_last_pcr_value)/27 ));

1766: 			pck.PTS = es->program->last_pcr_value;

1774: 				if ((diff_in_us<0) && (diff_in_us >= -200000)) {

No additional null check. But line 1044 has `gf_fatal_assert(pck->stream);` which checks pck->stream is not NULL. However, `pck->stream->program` could still be NULL.

So the fix narrows down the vulnerability to the ID3 handler. The question is: how does event 32 (ID3) get triggered with a NULL stream?

I think the answer lies in the section_complete function. For some table_id values that go through the `!sec->process_section` branch and have table_id 0xFC (TEMI timecode) at line 1301, event 30 is sent. But 0x74, 0x3A-0x3E, 0xFC, and the default (event 24) all use SL_PCK packets.

But wait, in the else branch (process_section is set), there's a switch for table_ids. PAT, PMT etc. have tables with table_id 0x00, 0x02, etc. For table_id 0x00 (PAT), the else branch sends event 24 (CAT_FOUND). 

Hmm, I think the vastdb explanation is WRONG about the event being ID3 or SCTE35. The actual event sent from section_complete with NULL ses is event 24 (CAT_FOUND), which doesn't do any harm.

But wait, let me reconsider the PCR path. The baseline says the crash is in the PCR handler at line 1055. But the cve.json says the source is the PAT gather_section with NULL. Are they connecting the NULL from the PAT path to the PCR handler somehow? 

Actually, rethinking... The baseline says the crash is at line 1055 in the PCR handler. It says the event path is:
1. PAT processing -> section_complete with NULL ses
2. section_complete sends on_event -> but this is event 24 (CAT_FOUND)
3. Then separately, PCR events are triggered in process_packet at line 1807

The baseline connects these by saying: "with NULL ses, creates packet with NULL stream and triggers on_event" and then skips to "PCR event is triggered". But these are TWO SEPARATE events. The first is event 24 from section_complete (which doesn't crash), and the second is event 13 (PES_PCR) from process_packet (line 1807).

In the PCR path, `pck.stream` is set from `es` which comes from program association. It should be non-NULL. But `es->program` could be NULL if the ES wasn't properly associated with a program.

OK, so both explanations might be partially right or wrong. Let me try to understand the actual vulnerability by looking at the fix.

The fix is ONLY in the ID3 handler at line 1196 (good.c): `if (!pck->stream) return;`. This prevents a NULL pointer dereference when `pck->stream` is NULL.

So the real question is: How does `pck->stream` become NULL in the ID3 event handler?

The ID3 event (event 32) is sent from section_complete... Let me check. In section_complete, event 32 (ID3) is NOT sent directly. It would be sent through some other path.

Actually, wait. Looking at the section_complete function for `!sec->process_section`:
- Case 0x74 (DSMCC): pck created, no event sent
- Case 0x3A-0x3E: pck created, no event sent  
- Case 0xFC (TEMI timecode): event 30 sent
- Default: event 24 sent

None of these send event 32 (ID3). So ID3 events must come from another path.

Looking at the process_packet function, the section processing path for IS_SECTION streams:
```c
} else if (es->flags & GF_M2TS_ES_IS_SECTION) {
    GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *ses = (GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *)es;
    if (ses->sec) gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ses->sec, ses, &hdr, data, payload_size);
}
```

Here, `ses` is non-NULL (it's derived from `es` which is a valid stream). So for section-based streams that have proper `es` pointers, the stream won't be NULL.

I think I may be overthinking this. Let me look at the actual real CVE-2024-6063 to understand the fix.

Wait, I can use webfetch to look up the CVE.

Actually, let me just reason based on what I have:

1. The ONLY difference between bad.c and good.c (based on my reading) is line 1196:
   - bad: `u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);`
   - good: `if (!pck->stream) return; u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);`

2. The fix adds a NULL check for `pck->stream` in the GF_M2TS_EVT_ID3 case handler.

3. The cve.json says the source is `gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ts->pat, NULL, &hdr, data, payload_size)` - the PAT path passes NULL for ses.

4. The sink is `u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams)` - which appears in ID3, SCTE35, and PCR handlers.

So the crash path must involve:
1. PAT processing with ses=NULL → section_complete called with ses=NULL
2. section_complete creates a packet with stream=NULL
3. This packet somehow triggers the ID3 event handler (event 32)

But looking at section_complete, event 32 is never sent directly. Section complete sends events 24, 30, or doesn't send anything.

Hmm, unless I'm wrong about which table_ids send event 32. Let me re-examine the switch in the else branch:

```c
if (ts->on_event) {
    switch (table_id) {
    case 0x00:
    case 0x42:
    case 0x02:
    case 0x40:
    case 0x70:
    case 0x73:
    {
        GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
        pck.data_len = sec->length;
        pck.data = sec->section;
        pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
        ts->on_event(ts, 24, &pck);
    }
    }
}
```

This is inside the `else` branch (process_section is set). So for tables like PAT, PMT, etc., this code ALSO runs. For PAT (table_id 0x00), it sends event 24 with stream = ses = NULL.

But wait, for PAT, the process_section callback (m2tsdmx_process_pat) is also called. That callback processes the PAT and creates programs/streams. Everything seems normal.

I think the issue might be this: when the section data contains table_id 0x00 (PAT) AND the process_section is set, the code sends event 24 with NULL stream alongside calling process_section. Event 24 = CAT_FOUND, which falls through without any action.

But what if different section filters without process_section receive data with table_id values that trigger different events? Actually no, the event numbers are hardcoded as 24 or 30.

OK, I think the real issue might be that there are MULTIPLE similarities between bad and good, and I need to check more carefully. Let me try to look at the exact diff between bad.c and good.c.

Hmm, actually I realize these are standalone test-case files with slightly different code. Let me check if there might be more differences by looking at specific point of the SCTE35 handler more carefully.

I already read the full files but they're very long. Let me use grep to find the key differences.

Actually, I can just look at this more carefully. From what I've read:

bad.c line 1191-1225: ID3 handler with no null check for pck->stream
good.c line 1191-1226: ID3 handler with null check `if (!pck->stream) return;`

bad.c line 1226-1261: SCTE35 handler with no null check
good.c line 1227-1262: SCTE35 handler with no null check (same code)

So the ONLY fix is the null check in the ID3 handler. This means the crash trigger is:
1. PAT processing with NULL ses
2. section_complete sends event 24 with stream=NULL
3. Event 24 (CAT_FOUND) falls through in m2tsdmx_on_event - does nothing
4. Some OTHER mechanism causes event 32 (ID3) to fire with a packet containing NULL stream

OR the actual path is different from what I'm thinking. Let me consider: maybe the ID3 data comes through a PID that's associated with the PAT section filter? No, that doesn't make sense.

Hmm, actually, thinking about this differently. In the section_complete function, when `!sec->process_section` is true (no process_section callback), the code sends events 24, 30, or nothing. But what if there's a section filter where process_section IS set AND it also has a table_id that causes a different event?

Looking at the condition at line 1288 (bad): `if (!sec->process_section)` - when process_section IS set, this block is skipped entirely and we go to the else branch. In the else branch, the event 24 dispatch happens, but THEN process_section is called too. So for PAT (which has process_section), event 24 is sent AND process_section is called.

For PAT with ses=NULL, event 24 is sent with stream=NULL, which falls through harmlessly.

I wonder if the actual vulnerability is different than what I'm thinking. Let me look at what the actual CVE is about.

You know what, let me just fetch the real CVE info online.

Actually let me just go with what I can observe:

1. The ONLY code difference is the `if (!pck->stream) return;` guard in the ID3 handler
2. The cve.json source is the PAT call with NULL ses
3. The sink is the dereference of stream->program->streams

The vastdb explanation says the crash is in ID3 or SCTE35 handlers. The baseline says the crash is in the PCR handler.

Looking at the code, the SCTE35 handler at line 1232 (bad) has `pck->stream->program->streams` - this has `GF_M2TS_SL_PCK *pck`, where `pck->stream` is `GF_M2TS_ES *`. The `stream` field in SL_PCK is `GF_M2TS_ES *stream`. So `pck->stream->program->streams` dereferences `stream`, then `program`, then `streams`.

For the ID3 handler, `pck->stream` is `GF_M2TS_PES *`, which is the same as `GF_M2TS_ES *` plus extra fields. `GF_M2TS_PES_PCK` has `GF_M2TS_PES *stream`. So `pck->stream->program->streams` also dereferences `stream`, `program`, and `streams`.

The PCR handler at line 1055 also does the same: `pck->stream->program->streams`.

All three handlers would crash if `pck->stream` is NULL. But the PCR handler has `gf_fatal_assert(pck->stream)` before the dereference, which means it would crash with an assertion failure (a controlled crash/exit) rather than a NULL pointer dereference. The fix for ID3 adds a protective return.

Now, the key question: which handler actually receives a NULL stream?

The fix only addresses the ID3 handler. This strongly suggests that the ID3 event handler is the one that receives NULL stream packets. But how?

Looking at the section_complete function again, event 32 (ID3) is never directly sent. ID3 data comes through specific PIDs with specific metadata. But when a section completes for a section filter that has no process_section, it sends event 24 or 30.

Wait, is there a section filter that handles ID3? ID3 data in MPEG-TS is typically carried through PID 0x0012 or other designated PIDs. When the `ts->ess[hdr.pid]` lookup finds an existing ES that's a section-based ES, it calls `gf_m2ts_gather_section` with `ses = (GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *)es` - this is non-NULL.

The only way to get a NULL `ses` through `gf_m2ts_gather_section` is via the system PID handlers:
- PAT: `ses = NULL`
- CAT: `ses = NULL`
- SDT/BAT: `ses = NULL`
- NIT: `ses = NULL`
- EIT: `ses = NULL`
- TDT/TOT: `ses = NULL`

These system tables use `ts->pat`, `ts->cat`, `ts->sdt`, `ts->nit`, `ts->eit`, `ts->tdt_tot` as the section filters. When their data completes, section_complete is called with ses=NULL.

For PAT, CAT, SDT, etc. that have process_section set:
- They go through the else branch
- Event 24 is sent with stream=NULL (for 0x00, 0x42, 0x02, 0x40, 0x70, 0x73)
- process_section is also called

For section filters without process_section (some might not have it):
- The `!sec->process_section` branch is taken
- For events without matching table_id: event 24 sent with stream=NULL

In ALL cases, the event sent is 24 (CAT_FOUND) NOT 32 (ID3). So how does ID3 get triggered with NULL stream?

I think the answer might be that the event 24 path DOES go through the ID3 handler somehow, or the explanation is slightly wrong about which event path is triggered.

Actually, you know what, let me re-read the vastdb explanation more carefully:

"gf_m2ts_section_complete (line 1583) ... Creates packet: pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses sets stream to NULL (line 1312) ... Calls event handler: ts->on_event(ts, 24, &pck) at line 1313"

"m2tsdmx_on_event ... Receives packet with NULL stream pointer from section_complete"

"bad.c:1196 (sink - ID3 handler) or bad.c:1232 (sink - SCTE35 handler) ... u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);"

So vastdb says event 24 is sent, which enters m2tsdmx_on_event. But then it claims the crash is in the ID3 or SCTE35 handler. But event 24 is CAT_FOUND, and there's no case for CAT_FOUND in m2tsdmx_on_event's switch. So it would fall through and NOT enter the ID3 or SCTE35 handlers.

So vastdb is WRONG about the trigger path. Event 24 does NOT enter the ID3 or SCTE35 handlers. Event 24 just falls through.

But wait, the cve.json says the sink is `gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams)` which appears in the ID3, SCTE35, and PCR handlers. The fix adds a null check ONLY in the ID3 handler. This means the crash must occur in the ID3 handler.

Is there a different call path where event 32 is sent with NULL stream? Let me think...

In `gf_m2ts_process_packet`, at the call to `gf_m2ts_gather_section`:
```c
} else if (es->flags & GF_M2TS_ES_IS_SECTION) {
    GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *ses = (GF_M2TS_SECTION_ES *)es;
    if (ses->sec) gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ses->sec, ses, &hdr, data, payload_size);
}
```

Here `ses` is derived from `es` which comes from `ts->ess[hdr.pid]`. This `es` should be non-NULL since we're in the `else` branch where `es` was found. So `ses` is non-NULL here. In this case, section_complete would create a packet with non-NULL stream. So the crash doesn't happen through this path.

Hmm, unless `es->program` is NULL even though `es` (and thus `ses`) is non-NULL. If `pck->stream` is non-NULL but `pck->stream->program` is NULL, then `pck->stream->program->streams` would be a NULL pointer dereference.

But the fix adds `if (!pck->stream) return;` - checking if stream is NULL, not checking if program is NULL. So the fix is for stream being NULL, not program being NULL.

OK I think there might be a path I'm missing. Let me consider this: In the PAT handler, `ses=NULL`. When section_complete is called for PAT, the event 24 is sent with stream=NULL. But maybe there's a different table_id that triggers event 32?

No, event 32 is never sent in section_complete. Event 24 is hardcoded.

Hmm, maybe the crash is not through the section_complete path but through the PCR handler path. Let me reconsider.

The cve.json "source" might just indicate where NULL is introduced, and the "sink" might be one of several places where it can crash. The fix might not correspond to the exact sink listed.

Actually, let me look at this from a totally different angle. Let me check if both bad.c and good.c have differences I haven't noticed.

Looking at the files line by line:
- bad.c line 1044: `gf_fatal_assert(pck->stream);`
- good.c line 1044: `gf_fatal_assert(pck->stream);` - same

- bad.c line 1196: `u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);`
- good.c line 1196: `if (!pck->stream) return;`
- good.c line 1197: `u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);`

So the fix is ONLY adding the null check in the ID3 handler.

Looking at cve.json:
- sink: `u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);`
This matches line 1196 in bad.c (ID3 handler), line 1055 (PCR handler), and line 1232 (SCTE35 handler).

But the fix is only in the ID3 handler. This could mean:
1. The actual crash only happens in the ID3 handler
2. Or the fix was a partial fix

Let me verify: is the PCR handler at line 1055 different between bad and good?

bad.c line 1055: `count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);`
good.c line 1055: `count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);`

Same. No fix in PCR handler.

bad.c line 1232: `u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);`
good.c line 1233: `u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);`

Same. No fix in SCTE35 handler.

So the fix is ONLY in the ID3 handler. This means the vulnerability trigger is specifically through the ID3 event handler receiving a packet with NULL stream.

For the ID3 handler to receive a packet with NULL stream, the event 32 (GF_M2TS_EVT_ID3) must be sent with a NULL stream pointer. Looking at where event 32 is sent... it's NOT sent in section_complete. It must be sent from somewhere else.

Hmm, event 32 is ID3. In gpac, ID3 metadata is typically carried in a separate PID. The ID3 section processing must have its own section filter. When that section filter's data is complete, section_complete is called.

But for the ID3 section filter, would process_section be set or not? If it's set, the section goes through the else branch, and the table_id might match one of the switch cases (0x00, 0x42, 0x02, 0x40, 0x70, 0x73), sending event 24. If the table_id doesn't match, no event is sent (the switch just falls through).

If process_section is NOT set, the section goes through the `!sec->process_section` branch. For table_id 0x74 (DSMCC), no event is sent. For 0x3A-0x3E (various descriptors), no event is sent. For 0xFC (TEMI), event 30 is sent. For any other table_id, event 24 is sent.

None of these send event 32! So how does event 32 ever get triggered?

Wait, I may be missing something. The events 24 and 30 sent from section_complete don't directly enter any meaningful handler in m2tsdmx_on_event. But the crucial thing is: what creates the actual crash?

OK, I think I've been overthinking this. Let me just look at what the actual real-world fix was. Let me search online for CVE-2024-6063.

Actually, let me look at the actual gpac commit 8767ed0 to see the patch.

Let me just check if there are other differences I might have missed by comparing the two files around key areas.

OK, I need to see if there are other differences beyond the ID3 handler. Let me do a more targeted comparison.

parts.text:
Now I can see the actual fix from the gpac commit 8767ed0. The commit says:

```
@@ -1211,6 +1211,9 @@ static void m2tsdmx_on_event(GF_M2TS_Demuxer *ts, u32 evt_type, void *param)
 1211		GF_BitStream *bs;
 1212		GF_M2TS_Prop *t;
 1213
1214+		//for now all SCTE35 must be associated with a stream
1215+		if (!pck->stream) return;
1216+
1217		// convey SCTE35 splice info to all streams of the program
1218		u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);
```

So the actual fix is in the SCTE35 handler! The fix adds `if (!pck->stream) return;` before `gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams)`.

But in my reading of good.c, line 1233 shows:
```c
u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);
```

And I didn't see a null check before it. Let me check good.c again around that area. I must have misread.

Let me look at good.c line 1233 more carefully. The test case might not have the exact same fix as the real commit.

<type>file</type>
<content>
1227: 	case GF_M2TS_EVT_SCTE35_SPLICE_INFO:
1228: 	{
1229: 		GF_M2TS_SL_PCK *pck = (GF_M2TS_SL_PCK*)param;
1230: 		void *bs;
1231: 		GF_M2TS_Prop *t;

Interesting! The good.c SCTE35 handler does NOT have the null check for `pck->stream`. But the actual GPAC fix (commit 8767ed0) adds the check in the SCTE35 handler. However, looking at the grep output earlier, the `if (!pck->stream) return;` check was found only at line 1196 in good.c, which is the ID3 handler.

So the test case has the fix in the ID3 handler (line 1196), not the SCTE35 handler. But the real commit fixes the SCTE35 handler.

This means the test case is a simplified version that doesn't exactly match the real fix location. The vulnerability trigger is the same though - NULL `pck->stream` being dereferenced.

Now let me look at the ID3 handler in both files to confirm:

baseline.reason: Correctly identifies the NULL propagation mechanism from PAT gather_section(ses=NULL) through section_complete into packet.stream, but incorrectly identifies the crash location as the PCR handler (line 1055). The fix is in the ID3 handler (line 1196), not the PCR handler. The PCR handler has a gf_fatal_assert(pck->stream) guard at line 1044, making it a controlled assertion failure, not a raw NULL dereference as described.

vastdb.reason: Correctly identifies the source (PAT gather_section with NULL ses), the propagation through section_complete setting pck.stream=NULL, and correctly identifies the ID3 handler (line 1196) as a vulnerable sink matching the test case fix location. However, claims event 24 (CAT_FOUND) from section_complete reaches the ID3 handler, which is wrong — event 24 has no case handler and falls through silently. Also incorrectly includes SCTE35 (line 1232) which is unfixed in this test case.

parts.text:
Now I can see the difference clearly:

bad.c (line 1196): `u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);`
good.c (lines 1196-1197): 
```c
if (!pck->stream) return;
u32 count = gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams);
```

So the fix in this test case is in the ID3 handler, adding `if (!pck->stream) return;`.

The real gpac commit 8767ed0 adds this fix in the SCTE35 handler, but in the test case, it's in the ID3 handler. This is likely because the test case was adapted/simplified.

Now let me understand the vulnerability:

1. The source is `gf_m2ts_gather_section(ts, ts->pat, NULL, &hdr, data, payload_size)` at bad.c:1676 - PAT processing with `ses = NULL`.

2. Inside `gf_m2ts_gather_section`, when the section is complete, it calls `gf_m2ts_section_complete(ts, sec, ses)` at line 1583, passing `ses = NULL`.

3. In `gf_m2ts_section_complete` (line 1278), the PAT table has `process_section` set, so it goes to the else branch. In the else branch, for table_id 0x00 (PAT), at line 1343-1347 (bad):
```c
GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
pck.data_len = sec->length;
pck.data = sec->section;
pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;  // ses is NULL
ts->on_event(ts, 24, &pck);
```
Event 24 (GF_M2TS_EVT_CAT_FOUND) is sent with pck.stream = NULL.

4. In `m2tsdmx_on_event`, event 24 (CAT_FOUND) doesn't have a case handler, so it falls through. No crash here.

Now, the ID3 event (32) must come through a different mechanism. Let me think about this...

Actually, looking at the section_complete function again, I notice the `!sec->process_section` branch. For section filters without process_section, the table_id 0xFC triggers event 30 (TEMI_TIMECODE), and other table_ids trigger event 24 (CAT_FOUND).

But event 32 (ID3) is never sent from section_complete. So where is it sent?

Wait, I need to look at this differently. In the `!sec->process_section` branch, the `ts->on_event(ts, 24, &pck)` is in the `else if (ts->on_event)` at the bottom. This is reached when the table_id doesn't match 0x74, 0x3A-0x3E, or 0xFC.

But what if the event handler treats event 24 differently than expected? Let me re-look at the switch cases in m2tsdmx_on_event...

Actually, I think the patch was originally for the SCTE35 handler. The test case adapted it to the ID3 handler. The underlying vulnerability mechanism is the same: a NULL stream pointer from section_complete reaches an event handler that dereferences it.

Let me now look at the actual trigger path more carefully.

The key insight is: when `gf_m2ts_gather_section` is called with `ses = NULL` for PAT (or other system tables), the `gf_m2ts_section_complete` function creates packets with `stream = NULL`. These packets are sent via `ts->on_event()`.

But which event type is sent? In section_complete:
- For the else branch (process_section is set): event 24 is sent for most table_ids
- For the !process_section branch: event 24 (default) or event 30 (for 0xFC)
- For DSMCC (0x74) and descriptors (0x3A-0x3E): no event is sent

Event 24 (CAT_FOUND) doesn't crash because there's no handler for it.

Now, for event 32 (ID3) to be triggered, we need a different flow. Maybe the ID3 section has its own process_section callback that calls ts->on_event(ts, GF_M2TS_EVT_ID3, ...)?

For section-based streams that have process_section set (like ID3), the section_complete function processes the table data through process_section callback. That callback sends event 32 with the section data and the ses pointer.

If the ID3 section filter was set up through a path where ses ends up being NULL, then event 32 would be sent with a NULL stream pointer.

But the question is: how would ses be NULL for the ID3 section filter?

Looking at the code flow:
1. PAT is processed with ses=NULL
2. PAT creates programs and maps PIDs
3. PMT is processed, creating streams and section filters
4. ID3 section filter is created with a valid ses pointer from ts->ess[]

So the ID3 section filter should have a valid ses pointer.

Unless... the vulnerability is not specifically about the ID3 event. Let me reconsider.

Actually, wait. Let me re-read the `!sec->process_section` branch more carefully.

```c
if (!sec->process_section) {
    if ((ts->on_event && (sec->section[0]==0x74)) ) {
        GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
        pck.data_len = sec->length;
        pck.data = sec->section;
        pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
        // NO on_event call - packet created but never sent
    } else if ((ts->on_event && (sec->section[0]==0x3A || sec->section[0]==0x3B ||
                                 sec->section[0]==0x3C || sec->section[0]==0x3D || sec->section[0]==0x3E)) ) {
        GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
        pck.data_len = sec->length;
        pck.data = sec->section;
        pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
        // NO on_event call
    }
    else if ((ts->on_event && (sec->section[0]==0xFC)) ) {
        GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
        pck.data_len = sec->length;
        pck.data = sec->section;
        pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
        ts->on_event(ts, 30, &pck);  // EVENT 30 = TEMI_TIMECODE
    }
    else if (ts->on_event) {
        GF_M2TS_SL_PCK pck;
        pck.data_len = sec->length;
        pck.data = sec->section;
        pck.stream = (GF_M2TS_ES *)ses;
        ts->on_event(ts, 24, &pck);  // EVENT 24 = CAT_FOUND
    }
}
```

So events 24 (CAT_FOUND) and 30 (TEMI_TIMECODE) are the only ones sent from this branch. Neither 31 (SCTE35) nor 32 (ID3).

For the else branch (process_section is set), the table-parsing code is executed, and then process_section callback is called. The process_section callback for various sections might internally call ts->on_event with appropriate event types. For example, the PAT section filter's process_section callback (m2tsdmx_process_pat) processes the PAT and might send events for PAT_FOUND, etc.

But looking at m2tsdmx_process_pat... I don't have the full code. It's declared as a static function in the original dmx_m2ts.c but isn't included in the test case.

Actually, looking at the test case structure, the m2tsdmx_on_event function is the key event handler. The events come from the outside (from the demuxer library code, not shown in the test case).

OK, I think I've spent too long analyzing the exact trigger path. Let me just summarize what I know:

1. The vulnerability is a NULL pointer dereference (CWE-476) in gpac's MPEG-TS demuxer
2. The root cause is that `gf_m2ts_gather_section` is called with `ses=NULL` for PAT (and other system tables), and this NULL propagates through to packet stream pointers
3. When the section is complete, the NULL ses is assigned to `pck.stream`
4. The event handler (`m2tsdmx_on_event`) receives this packet and processes it in various event cases
5. Some event handlers (ID3, SCTE35, PCR) access `pck->stream->program->streams` without checking if `pck->stream` is NULL
6. The fix adds a NULL check for `pck->stream` before dereferencing it

For the SCTE35 handler specifically (in the real fix), or the ID3 handler (in this test case), the NULL stream is dereferenced, causing the crash.

Now let me evaluate the two explanations:

**Baseline explanation:**
- Root Cause: Correctly identifies NULL pointer dereference. Says the crash is in the PCR handler at line 1055 when `pck->stream->program` is NULL. But the fix is in the ID3 handler, not the PCR handler.
- The baseline says the PCR handler access `program->streams` without checking if program is NULL. But the test case fix is for `pck->stream` being NULL in the ID3 handler.
- The baseline mentions both the PAT processing path AND the PCR path, but they're different events.
- The baseline claims the crash is at line 1055 in the PCR handler. But the cve sink is `gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams)` which appears in ID3 (line 1196) and SCTE35 (line 1232) handlers too.
- Score: Partially correct but misidentifies the specific event handler path (PCR vs ID3). The PCR handler has an assert at line 1044 that would catch NULL stream, protecting against that specific crash.

**VASTdb explanation:**

- Says the sink is at lines 1196 (ID3) and 1232 (SCTE35)
- The fix in this test case IS at line 1196 (ID3), so vastdb correctly identifies the ID3 handler
- But vastdb also mentions SCTE35 (line 1232), which isn't fixed in this test case
- Vastdb mentions that event 24 is sent from section_complete at line 1313, but event 24 is CAT_FOUND, not ID3 or SCTE35. This is a weakness in the explanation because event 24 wouldn't trigger the ID3 or SCTE35 handlers.
- Vastdb identifies the correct location (ID3 handler) but has some confusion in the trigger path (saying event 24 leads to ID3 handler)

Wait, actually, the vastdb explanation says:
- The section_complete sends event 24 (from line 1313)
- Then the event enters m2tsdmx_on_event
- Then it says the sink is at line 1196 (ID3) or line 1232 (SCTE35)

But event 24 is CAT_FOUND which has no case handler. So it wouldn't reach the ID3 or SCTE35 handlers. This is a significant error in the trigger path.

However, the vastdb explanation correctly identifies:
1. The source: PAT gather_section with NULL ses
2. The propagation: section_complete assigns NULL to pck.stream
3. The vulnerable handlers: ID3 and/or SCTE35 (both have the same dereference pattern)
4. The sink: `gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams)`

The baseline explanation says the crash is in the PCR handler (event 13), not ID3 (event 32) or SCTE35 (event 31). But the PCR handler has an assert guard. And the fix is in the ID3 handler. So the baseline is incorrect about which handler is vulnerable.

Let me re-evaluate:

For the baseline:
- It identifies the NULL dereference correctly (root cause)
- It says the crash is in the PCR handler (line 1055) - but the fix is in ID3 handler
- The trigger path includes PAT processing with NULL (correct) but then connects to PCR event (incorrect)
- The assertion at line 1044 would trigger for NULL stream in PCR handler, which is a controlled crash/exit, not a silent NULL pointer dereference
- Overall, the baseline correctly identifies:
  - The mechanism (NULL ses from PAT gather_section → section_complete → packet with NULL stream)
  - The vulnerability type (NULL pointer dereference)
- But incorrectly identifies the specific crash location (PCR vs ID3)

For the vastdb:
- Correctly identifies the source: PAT gather_section with NULL ses
- Correctly identifies the propagation through section_complete
- Correctly identifies the ID3 handler as vulnerable (and SCTE35 too)
- But the trigger path says event 24 (CAT_FOUND) reaches ID3 handler, which is incorrect - event 24 doesn't match any case
- Event 24 being sent from section_complete is correct, but it's unclear how event 32 (ID3) would be triggered

Both explanations have accuracy issues but correctly identify the core mechanism.

Now, actually, thinking about it more carefully: the event 24 (CAT_FOUND) is sent from section_complete. But maybe there's a different path I'm not seeing. Let me check if the test case has any code that converts event 24 into something else... No, the switch statement just falls through for unhandled cases.

So both explanations have a weakness in the trigger path. But vastdb correctly identifies the ID3 handler as the vulnerable location (matching the test case fix), while baseline identifies the PCR handler (incorrect).

Let me also note: the cve.json says the source is PAT gather_section with NULL, and the sink is `gf_list_count(pck->stream->program->streams)`. This sink appears in all three handlers. The fix in the test case is in the ID3 handler.

I think the real scenario is:
1. A crafted MPEG-TS stream triggers section completion with NULL ses
2. The NULL propagates through packet creation
3. Depending on the crafted data, different event handlers might be invoked with the NULL stream
4. The ID3 handler (in this test case) is one such handler that crashes without the null check

- `ts->on_event(ts, 24, &pck)` at line 1313 (bad) and 1347 (bad)

These send event 24 (GF_M2TS_EVT_CAT_FOUND based on my counting). But in m2tsdmx_on_event, event 24 is NOT handled, so it falls through.
